title: Flags and Loops
lesson:
The 8080 keeps a flag register that records facts about the last result: Zero (Z), Sign (S), Carry (CY), Parity (P), and Auxiliary Carry (AC). Arithmetic and logic instructions set these automatically, and conditional jumps read them. JNZ jumps while Z is clear, JZ while it's set, JC on a carry, and so on.

The everyday loop pattern is DCR (decrement a register, which sets the flags) followed by JNZ back to the top, repeating until the counter reaches zero. It's the same idea as the PDP-8's ISZ loop and the 6502's DEX and BNE you'll meet next: a counter, a test, a branch. Once you can spot it, you can read a loop in any assembly language.
prompt: Work out 5 plus 4 plus 3 plus 2 plus 1, which is 15. Keep the running sum in A and a counter in B (start it at 5). Each pass, add B to A and DCR B, looping with JNZ while B is not zero.
starterCode:
MVI A, 0     ; sum = 0
        MVI B, 5     ; counter = 5
LOOP:   ADD B        ; A = A + B
        DCR B        ; B = B - 1 (sets the Zero flag when it hits 0)
        JNZ LOOP     ; loop while B is not 0
        HLT
hints: DCR B decrements B and updates the flags.
JNZ LOOP jumps back as long as the last result wasn't zero, so the loop ends when B reaches 0.
Add B before decrementing it: 5 plus 4 plus 3 plus 2 plus 1 is 15.
successText: Register A must equal 15 at the halt.
reference:
MVI A, 0
MVI B, 5
LOOP: ADD B
DCR B
JNZ LOOP
HLT
